data.four_set_effect: Increases Charged Attack DMG by 35% if the character uses a Catalyst or a Bow.

circlet_of_logos.desc: A top hat that has managed to retain its radiance despite braving the elements for time untold. An ancient tune still resonates within.
circlet_of_logos.title: Conductor's Top Hat

flower_of_life.desc: A small flower-shaped insignia. If you listen carefully, you can almost hear a flute playing and voices singing.
flower_of_life.title: Troupe's Dawnlight

goblet_of_eonothem.desc: An ancient, strangely-shaped canteen. The interior is fitted with harp strings, which play a wondrous tune as the water flows out.
goblet_of_eonothem.title: Wanderer's String-Kettle

plume_of_death.desc: An azure arrow fletching that has neither faded nor splintered with the passage of time. The sound of running water seems to linger around it.
plume_of_death.title: Bard's Arrow Feather

sands_of_eon.desc: An hourglass used to keep time during a band performance. It once made a crisp sound, but their performance has since ended.
sands_of_eon.title: Concert's Final Hour

list.desc: A small flower-shaped insignia. There seems to be music coming from it.</br>Among the members of the troupe was a charismatic swordswoman.</br>Beautiful as the light on water, elegant as the chirping lark.</br>Her every slash hummed a tune of the wind.</br>The end of each song and dance was like the sun after the rain.</br>Dust has since settled, and both the stage and life itself feel emptier for the absence of her music.</br>Both her music and her sword were as lethal as they were beautiful.</br>This was in keeping with the troupe's performances, which were intended for two very different audiences.</br>For the audience of foes, the music drifted far beyond the stage.</br>
list.title: Troupe's Dawnlight

list.desc: A beautiful top hat untouched by time.</br>Listening closely, one can almost hear an ancient tune echoing inside the hat.</br>Thousands of years ago, a troupe wandered through the lands.</br>The troupe owned not a single shred of sheet music. What they saw was what they sung, and what they heard was what they played.</br>As time passed, the troupe gradually realized the vastness of the world.</br>"There are so many things in the world for us to sing of."</br>The members then turned the notes into words and recorded their journey in a book.</br>This book, The Widsith as they called it, was still held tightly in the conductor's hands even after his death.</br>
list.title: Conductor's Top Hat

list.desc: A strangely-shaped kettle that plays a wondrous tune as the water flows out.</br>There was always music in the life of a Troupe member, even when drinking water.</br>An ancient tale tells of a wandering troupe that roamed the land.</br>For flutes they had swords, and for harps they had bows. They performed not only for friends, but also for foes.</br>They traveled through the desert and set foot in the blazing Mare Jivari.</br>The tune of the harp echoing in the kettle was a reminder for them.</br>"Along with the boundless tunes we walk."</br>"Wherever there is music, there is us."</br>
list.title: Wanderer's String-Kettle

list.desc: An azure arrow fletching that has survived the test of time.</br>The fletching seems to produce a tune as the arrow flies through air.</br>The harpist of the troupe was also a skilled archer.</br>Legend has it that he could lure the birds in the sky with his harp and then shoot them down with his arrows.</br>It is said that he never looked at the sky while he was playing the harp to lure the birds.</br>Some believed the reason was his harpist's arrogance, while his companion said it was his hunter's mercy.</br>The ill-fated birds became the fletching of the harpist's arrows,</br>and the sound of the harp became the birds' elegy.</br>
list.title: Bard's Arrow Feather

list.desc: The troupe's hourglass, which is also a harp.</br>The tune becomes deeper with the passage of time.</br>When the performance was coming to an end, the troupe would play this harp.</br>As time passed, the tune of the harp became deeper.</br>The sound of the harp dying down marked the end of the show.</br>Everything has an end, and the troupe was no exception.</br>One by one, each member of the troupe met their fate and their instruments were buried deep in the dust.</br>As the harp fell silent, the final sound heard from the musical troupe was the faint trickle of sand as it slid down the hourglass one final time.</br>
list.title: Concert's Final Hour
